title: Substring with At Least K Repeats and Swaps

statement.markdown: Given a string `s` and two integers `k` and `n`, find the length of the longest substring `t` that has at least `k` distinct characters and at most `n` character swaps. A character swap is defined as swapping two adjacent characters in the substring.
statement.inputFormat: Input string `s` with length up to 10000 characters, and two integers `k` and `n` (1 <= k <= 100, 1 <= n <= 10000)
statement.outputFormat: The length of the longest substring `t` with at least `k` distinct characters and at most `n` character swaps
statement.notes: A character swap is defined as swapping two adjacent characters in the substring.

constraints.description: Minimum number of distinct characters in substring

examples.explanation: One possible substring is "abc" with 3 characters and 0 swaps. Another possible substring is "bca" with 3 characters and 1 swap.
One possible substring is "aaaaaa" with 1 character (a) and 0 swaps.

hints.text: Start with an empty string and expand it to the longest possible substring
Use a sliding window approach to find the longest substring with at least k distinct characters
Swap characters in the window to minimize the number of swaps and find the longest substring

editorial.title: Substring with At Least K Repeats and Swaps
editorial.intuition: The idea is to start with an empty string and expand it to the longest possible substring. We use a sliding window approach to find the longest substring with at least k distinct characters. Then, we perform character swaps in the window to minimize the number of swaps and find the longest substring.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to generate all possible substrings and check if each substring has at least k distinct characters and at most n character swaps. However, this approach is inefficient and will exceed the time limit.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a sliding window approach to find the longest substring with at least k distinct characters. Then, we perform character swaps in the window to minimize the number of swaps and find the longest substring.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to start with an empty string and expand it to the longest possible substring. We use a sliding window approach to find the longest substring with at least k distinct characters. Then, we perform character swaps in the window to minimize the number of swaps and find the longest substring. We use a set to keep track of unique characters in the window and a variable to keep track of the number of swaps.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that the optimal solution always finds the longest substring with at least k distinct characters and at most n character swaps.

commonMistakes: Failing to account for character swaps
Not using a set to keep track of unique characters

functionSignature.java: int solve(String s, int k, int n);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, s: str, k: int, n: int) -> int:
functionSignature.javascript: int solve(string s, int k, int n);
